item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

media:content.media:title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss
media:content.media:description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

item.title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss

item.dc:creator: Matt Dixon
item.description: President Donald Trump isn’t on the ballot this year, but with roughly six months before Election Day, he’ll still likely be the biggest factor in determining the fortunes of the Republican Party

media:content.media:text: President Trump Hosts Small Business Summit
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during a small-business summit in the East Room of the White House on May 4.

item.title: What GOP officials are hopeful — and worried — about six months from the midterms

item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

media:content.media:title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss
media:content.media:description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

item.title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss

item.dc:creator: Brennan Leach
Melanie Zanona
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy’s political career is at stake on Saturday as Louisiana voters weigh in on the Republican for the first time since he voted to convict President Donald Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial

media:content.media:credit: Getty Images; Bloomberg via Getty Images; AP
media:content.media:text: Rep. Julia Letlow, Sen. Bill Cassidy, and Louisiana state Treasurer John Fleming.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Julia Letlow, Sen. Bill Cassidy, and Louisiana state Treasurer John Fleming.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy faces a primary for the first time since his Trump impeachment vote

item.dc:creator: Shira Pinson
Matt Bradley
item.description: Calls for Jewish settlements in Gaza are shifting toward the mainstream in Israel, with some groups advocating the wholesale ethnic cleansing of Palestinians.

media:content.media:text: Image: Israelis Observe Yom Ha'atzmaut, National Independence Day
media:content.media:description: Right-wing Nachala movement settlers march near the Gaza border on April 22, 2026 near Kibbutz Nir Am, Israel.

media:content.media:text: PALESTINIAN-ISRAEL-CONFLICT-SETTLEMENTS
media:content.media:description: Israel's Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich, center, surrounded by Israeli settlers at the end of the resettlement ceremony of Sa-Nur, south of Jenin, in the Israeli-occupied West Bank on Apr. 19.

media:content.media:text: Daily life of displaced Palestinians among rubbles of the Islamic University in Gaza
media:content.media:description: Displaced Palestinian families live in makeshift tents and damaged buildings within the campus of the Islamic University of Gaza, which was destroyed by Israeli attacks, pictured on April 12.

media:content.media:text: Israelis Observe Yom Ha'atzmaut, National Independence Day
media:content.media:description: Nachala movement leader Daniella Weiss gives a speech near Kibbutz Nir Am, Israel, on Apr. 22.

item.title: Extremist Jewish settlers eye Gaza as they seek to expel Palestinians from enclave

item.dc:creator: Denise Chow
item.description: SpaceX plans to launch a new version of its Starship rocket — a prototype of the system that NASA hopes will carry its astronauts to the moon — on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Starship V3 on the launch pad during a wet dress rehearsal.
media:content.media:description: Starship V3 on the launchpad during a rehearsal.

item.title: For SpaceX, the stakes of next week’s Starship rocket test flight are sky-high

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: Mustafe Denbil runs an agricultural shop and farm in northern Somalia, but three months after ordering fertilizer and pesticides, the supplies his business needs are still stranded in Dubai

media:content.media:text: Humanitarian crisis deepens in Somalia after Strait of Hormuz closure
media:content.media:description: Residents of the Maacani Camp in Mogadishu, Somalia are struggling to survive in tents under limited conditions as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz cause serious disruptions in the passage of ships on May 5.

item.title: How the Middle East conflict is worsening Somalia’s slide into famine

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: A GOP bill seeking $1 billion for the Secret Service to help finance President Donald Trump’s White House ballroom is in jeopardy as it faces pushback from a top Senate official.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump holds an artist's rendering of the new White House ballroom on Oct. 22, 2025.
media:content.media:description: The parliamentarian wrote that the bill would be subject to a 60-vote threshold to pass, meaning it can’t move forward with a simple majority as similar bills advanced using budget reconciliation.

item.title: Senate parliamentarian nixes Trump’s ballroom fund in budget bill

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Before winning the 151st Preakness Stakes, Napoleon Solo almost wasn’t entered in the race known as the middle jewel of horse racing’s Triple Crown

media:content.media:text: Three men smile while holding silver trophies high into the air.
media:content.media:description: Horse trainer Chad Summers, right, celebrating Napoleon Solo's win with jockey Paco Lopez and owner Al Gold following the 151st Preakness Stakes at Laurel Park on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A jockey, seen from behind, raising a fist in triumph while riding a gray horse.
media:content.media:description: Horse trainer Chad Summers, right, celebrating Napoleon Solo's win with jockey Paco Lopez and owner Al Gold following the 151st Preakness Stakes at Laurel Park on Saturday.

item.title: A horse almost didn’t race in the Preakness. Thanks to a jockey’s suggestion, it won.

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Mustafa Fattah
item.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

media:content.media:text: Photo illustrations of maggots alongside surgical equipment
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Polly Cleveland
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Medicinal maggots in a sachet.
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Medicinal maggots in a petri dish.
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

item.title: Got maggots? These doctors are bringing the bugs into their practice on purpose

item.dc:creator: Denise Chow
item.description: SpaceX plans to launch a new version of its Starship rocket — a prototype of the system that NASA hopes will carry its astronauts to the moon — on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Starship V3 on the launch pad during a wet dress rehearsal.
media:content.media:description: Starship V3 on the launchpad during a rehearsal.

item.title: For SpaceX, the stakes of next week’s Starship rocket test flight are sky-high

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: Mustafe Denbil runs an agricultural shop and farm in northern Somalia, but three months after ordering fertilizer and pesticides, the supplies his business needs are still stranded in Dubai

media:content.media:text: Humanitarian crisis deepens in Somalia after Strait of Hormuz closure
media:content.media:description: Residents of the Maacani Camp in Mogadishu, Somalia are struggling to survive in tents under limited conditions as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz cause serious disruptions in the passage of ships on May 5.

item.title: How the Middle East conflict is worsening Somalia’s slide into famine

item.dc:creator: Erika Edwards
item.description: A recent suspected hantavirus case in Illinois is a stark reminder that the potentially deadly virus does exist in the U.S.There are currently no cases of hantavirus in the U.S. that are linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak.

media:content.media:text: A deer mouse.
media:content.media:description: Hantavirus is carried by various rodents, especially the deer mouse. The strains of hantavirus in the U.S. don't spread from person to person.

item.title: Where does hantavirus lurk in the U.S.? Scientists found ‘hot spots’ in surprising areas

item.dc:creator: Matt Dixon
item.description: President Donald Trump isn’t on the ballot this year, but with roughly six months before Election Day, he’ll still likely be the biggest factor in determining the fortunes of the Republican Party

media:content.media:text: President Trump Hosts Small Business Summit
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during a small-business summit in the East Room of the White House on May 4.

item.title: What GOP officials are hopeful — and worried — about six months from the midterms

item.description: Calls for Jewish settlements in Gaza are shifting toward the mainstream in Israel, with some groups advocating the wholesale ethnic cleansing of Palestinians.

media:content.media:text: Image: Israelis Observe Yom Ha'atzmaut, National Independence Day
media:content.media:description: Right-wing Nachala movement settlers march near the Gaza border on April 22, 2026 near Kibbutz Nir Am, Israel.

media:content.media:text: PALESTINIAN-ISRAEL-CONFLICT-SETTLEMENTS

media:content.media:description: Displaced Palestinian families live in makeshift tents and damaged buildings within the campus of the Islamic University of Gaza, which was destroyed by Israeli attacks, pictured on April 12.

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
item.description: Competing messages from candidates like Bob Brooks and Ryan Crosswell highlight a battle for the soul of the Democratic Party in a swing district in Pennsylvania.

media:content.media:text: Ryan Crosswell; Bob Brooks
media:content.media:description: Ryan Crosswell, left, and Bob Brooks are candidates for the Democratic nomination in Pennsylvania's 7th Congressional District.

media:content.media:text: Carol Obando-Derstine
media:content.media:description: Ryan Crosswell, left, and Bob Brooks are candidates for the Democratic nomination in Pennsylvania's 7th Congressional District.

media:content.media:text: Lamont McClure
media:content.media:description: Ryan Crosswell, left, and Bob Brooks are candidates for the Democratic nomination in Pennsylvania's 7th Congressional District.

item.title: The House primary giving hints about where the national Democratic Party is heading

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: A GOP bill seeking $1 billion for the Secret Service to help finance President Donald Trump’s White House ballroom is in jeopardy as it faces pushback from a top Senate official.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump holds an artist's rendering of the new White House ballroom on Oct. 22, 2025.
media:content.media:description: The parliamentarian wrote that the bill would be subject to a 60-vote threshold to pass, meaning it can’t move forward with a simple majority as similar bills advanced using budget reconciliation.

item.title: Senate parliamentarian nixes Trump’s ballroom fund in budget bill

item.dc:creator: Greg Rosenstein
item.description: MMA superstar Conor McGregor will return to the octagon for the first time since 2021 to face Max Holloway on July 11 in Las Vegas, UFC President Dana White announced Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Conor McGregor at UFC 264 event on July 10, 2021, in Las Vegas, Nevada.
media:content.media:description: Conor McGregor at UFC 264 event on July 10, 2021, in Las Vegas, Nevada.

item.title: UFC star Conor McGregor returning to the cage for first time in 5 years in July

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy’s primary loss Saturday brings an end to a two-decade career in public office that was ultimately defined by tensions with President Donald Trump

media:content.media:text: Bill Cassidy.
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bill Cassidy at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 15.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy spent 20 years in public office. One decision led to his demise.

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
Brennan Leach
Melanie Zanona
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict President Donald Trump on impeachment charges in 2021, has lost his Republican primary in Louisiana, as two challengers aligned with Trump advanced to a runoff.Rep.

media:content.media:text: Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., during a Senate hearing on Sept. 17, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., during a Senate hearing on Sept. 17, 2025.

media:content.media:text: Parents Bill of Rights
media:content.media:description: Rep. Julia Letlow, R-La., at the U.S. Capitol in 2023.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses GOP primary in Louisiana, as two rivals advance to a runoff

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Before winning the 151st Preakness Stakes, Napoleon Solo almost wasn’t entered in the race known as the middle jewel of horse racing’s Triple Crown

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Quarterback Aaron Rodgers is returning to the Pittsburgh Steelers for a 22nd season in the NFL, according to multiple reports

media:content.media:text: Pittsburgh Steelers quarterback Aaron Rodgers tries to quiet the crowd during a game against the Baltimore Ravens on Jan. 4, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Pittsburgh Steelers quarterback Aaron Rodgers tries to quiet the crowd during a game against the Baltimore Ravens on Jan. 4.

media:content.media:text: Image: Buffalo Bills v New York Jets Aaron Rodgers
media:content.media:description: Pittsburgh Steelers quarterback Aaron Rodgers tries to quiet the crowd during a game against the Baltimore Ravens on Jan. 4.

item.title: Aaron Rodgers returning to Steelers for 22nd NFL season, per reports

item.dc:creator: Andrew Jones
item.description: VIENNA — Bulgaria triumphed on Saturday night at the Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna, held under the shadow of controversy over the continued participation of Israel

media:content.media:text: Dara holds a glass trophy into the air as gold confetti falls on the stage.
media:content.media:description: Dara of Bulgaria celebrates on stage while holding the trophy during the Grand Final of the 70th Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna, Austria on May 17, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Darina Nikolaeva Yotova performing on a darkened stage with magenta and purple lighting.
media:content.media:description: Dara performing during a dress rehearsal in Vienna on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Hundreds of protestors in a crowd holding signs condemning genocide.
media:content.media:description: A demonstration on the sidelines of the Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Noam Bettan.
media:content.media:description: Noam Bettan from Israel with the song "Michelle" performs during the rehearsal for the final of the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Eurovision Song Contest - Rehearsal for the final
media:content.media:description: Linda Lampenius from Finland performs "Liekinheitin" during the rehearsal for the final of the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Friday.

item.title: Bulgaria wins the Eurovision Song Contest with Dara’s ‘Bangaranga’

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: Save on outdoor throw pillows, gardening supplies at Walmart right now — including finds for as low as $3.
item.title: 12+ under-$25 outdoor deals to shop at Walmart this weekend
